subject: Re: WarpTools crashing on frame motion correction
body:
Hi Alister,

I got my IT department to check in on the installation and they discovered 
that Warp was unable to connect to the internet the way it was installed 
into our HPC environment. After modifying to give internet access, I was 
able to successfully run the tutorial data.

I'm having some other problems with running it on my own data, but I will 
troubleshoot for some more time to see if I can solve it on my own.

Thanks,
Daniel

On Thursday, July 25, 2024 at 9:19:18 AM UTC-4 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi both,
>
> @both which version(s) of Warp are you attempting to run? and on which 
> cards?
>
> We recently moved from CUDA 11.7 to 11.8 
> <https://github.com/warpem/warp/pull/184> which bumped the maximum 
> compute capability from 8.6 to 9.0 (useful table 
> <https://stackoverflow.com/questions/28932864/which-compute-capability-is-supported-by-which-cuda-versions/28933055#28933055>
> ).
>
> @Jesse we've run workshops on A10 cards (CC 8.6) without issue so I think 
> there may be something up with your installation.
> The 11GB memory on the 1080Ti is likely to limit you for big refinements 
> indeed, unfortunately those cards are a bit behind the times. You could try 
> --cpu_memory in MCore if forced to used these cards for whatever reason.
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
>
> On Wed, 24 Jul 2024 at 09:52, Jesse Hansen <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> I believe I was getting similar errors when testing on different nodes.
>>
>> for what it's worth, I have found warptools is finicky with which nodes 
>> it will run on in our cluster.  I am still trying to track down the 
>> pattern, but currently it only runs with full functionality on our node 
>> with an NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti. 
>>
>> I also tried on a node with Nvidia A10 and it did not run. I ran the 
>> exact same job on the node above and it worked.
>>
>> I tested on GeForce GTX 1080 Ti and it worked okay for warptools but not 
>> mtools.  
>>
>> best,
>>
>> Jesse
>> On Tuesday, July 23, 2024 at 11:40:59 PM UTC+2 dxh...@gmail.com wrote:
>>
>>> As an update, I ran the tutorial verbatim with the tutorial data and it 
>>> still produced the same error. Could this be a problem with the 
>>> installation?
>>>
>>> On Tuesday, July 23, 2024 at 10:47:24 AM UTC-4 Daniel Cai wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Everyone,
>>>>
>>>> I've been trying to learn warp following the online tutorial with my 
>>>> own data, but sadly encountered a very early error.
>>>> Warp can't connect to workers on the step with motion correction and 
>>>> averaging of the frames. It's able to find the frames but unable to run. I 
>>>> tried changing between .mrc and .tif frame files and modifying the number 
>>>> of processes/gpu, and running it on a more powerful gpu, but haven't had 
>>>> any luck so far.
>>>> I've copied the error below.
>>>>
>>>> Does anyone have a clue why this might be the case? 
>>>>
>>>> Thanks in advance!
>>>> Daniel
>>>>
>>>> Unhandled exception. System.Exception: Couldn't connect to newly 
>>>> created worker at localhost:36779 for 20 seconds, something must be wrong
>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper..ctor(Int32 deviceID, Boolean silent, Boolean 
>>>> attachDebugger) in /opt/warp/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 104
>>>>    at WarpTools.Commands.DistributedOptions.GetWorkers(Boolean 
>>>> attachDebugger) in /opt/warp/WarpTools/Commands/DistributedOptions.cs:line 
>>>> 57
>>>>    at WarpTools.Commands.MotionCTFFrameseries.Run(Object options) in 
>>>> /opt/warp/WarpTools/Commands/Frameseries/MotionCTFFrameseries.cs:line 187
>>>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.Run(Object options) in 
>>>> /opt/warp/WarpTools/Program.cs:line 30
>>>>    at Warp.Tools.CommandLineParserHelper.ParseAndRun(String[] args, 
>>>> Func`2 run, Type[] verbs, String appName) in 
>>>> /opt/warp/WarpLib/Tools/CommandLineParserHelper.cs:line 26
>>>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.Main(String[] args) in 
>>>> /opt/warp/WarpTools/Program.cs:line 17
>>>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.<Main>(String[] args)
>>>> /usr/local/apps/warp/2.0.0dev11/bin/WarpTools: line 10: 846294 Aborted 
>>>>                 singularity run ${nv_opt} "$(dirname $(readlink -f 
>>>> ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}))/warp_2.0.0dev11.sif" "$(basename $0)" "$@"
>>>>
>>> --

>> "Warp" group.
